Role.title: Role
Role.description: Represents user roles within the system for access control and defining permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

name.description: The name of the role (e.g., 'Admin', 'Warehouse Manager', 'Operator').

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the OmniStock system, linked to a specific role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: Unique username for the user, used for login.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

roleId.description: Reference to the Role assigned to this user. (Relationship: Role 1:N User)

isActive.description: Indicates if the user account is active. Inactive users cannot log in.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Warehouse.title: Warehouse
Warehouse.description: Represents a physical or logical location where products are stored and managed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Warehouse entity.

name.description: The unique name of the warehouse.

address.description: The physical address of the warehouse.

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User who is responsible for this warehouse. (Relationship: User 1:N Warehouse)

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the warehouse.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the warehouse has been soft-deleted.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the warehouse was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the warehouse was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification category for products, used to organize inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Groceries').

description.description: An optional description of the category.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product managed within the inventory system, including its details and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

unitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the product is measured (e.g., 'pcs', 'kg', 'liter').

costPrice.description: The average cost price of the product.

salePrice.description: The selling price of the product.

barcode.description: An optional barcode or QR code identifier for the product.

lowStockThreshold.description: The minimum quantity for this product across all warehouses, triggering a low-stock alert if total falls below.

isDeleted.description: Flag indicating if the product has been soft-deleted.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Represents a supplier from whom products are purchased and received.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

name.description: The company name of the supplier.

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person at the supplier.

phoneNumber.description: The main contact phone number for the supplier.

email.description: The email address of the supplier.

address.description: The physical address of the supplier.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was last updated.

GoodsReceipt.title: GoodsReceipt
GoodsReceipt.description: Records the process of receiving goods into a specific warehouse (Stock In).

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsReceipt entity.

deliveryNoteNumber.description: The unique number from the supplier's delivery note or nakladnoy.

receiptDate.description: The date and time when the goods were received.

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier from whom the goods were received. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N GoodsReceipt)

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse where the goods were received. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N GoodsReceipt)

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User who processed this goods receipt. (Relationship: User 1:N GoodsReceipt)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the goods receipt record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the goods receipt record was last updated.

GoodsReceiptItem.title: GoodsReceiptItem
GoodsReceiptItem.description: Represents an individual product and its quantity received as part of a specific goods receipt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsReceiptItem entity.

goodsReceiptId.description: Reference to the GoodsReceipt this item belongs to. (Relationship: GoodsReceipt 1:N GoodsReceiptItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was received. (Relationship: Product 1:N GoodsReceiptItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product received.

unitPrice.description: The unit price of the product at the time of receipt.

GoodsIssue.title: GoodsIssue
GoodsIssue.description: Records the process of issuing goods from a specific warehouse (Stock Out).

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsIssue entity.

issueDate.description: The date and time when the goods were issued.

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse from which the goods were issued. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N GoodsIssue)

destination.description: A description of the destination for the issued goods (e.g., client name, internal use).

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User who processed this goods issue. (Relationship: User 1:N GoodsIssue)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the goods issue record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the goods issue record was last updated.

GoodsIssueItem.title: GoodsIssueItem
GoodsIssueItem.description: Represents an individual product and its quantity issued as part of a specific goods issue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsIssueItem entity.

goodsIssueId.description: Reference to the GoodsIssue this item belongs to. (Relationship: GoodsIssue 1:N GoodsIssueItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was issued. (Relationship: Product 1:N GoodsIssueItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product issued.

Transfer.title: Transfer
Transfer.description: Records the movement of goods between two different warehouses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transfer entity.

transferDate.description: The date and time when the transfer was initiated.

sourceWarehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse from which goods are transferred. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N Transfer (Source))

destinationWarehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse to which goods are transferred. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N Transfer (Destination))

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User who initiated or approved the transfer. (Relationship: User 1:N Transfer)

status.description: The current status of the transfer (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Transit', 'Completed', 'Canceled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transfer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transfer record was last updated.

TransferItem.title: TransferItem
TransferItem.description: Represents an individual product and its quantity being transferred as part of a specific warehouse transfer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransferItem entity.

transferId.description: Reference to the Transfer this item belongs to. (Relationship: Transfer 1:N TransferItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being transferred. (Relationship: Product 1:N TransferItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product being transferred.

WarehouseProductStock.title: WarehouseProductStock
WarehouseProductStock.description: Stores the current stock balance for a specific product within a specific warehouse, enabling real-time stock tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WarehouseProductStock entity.

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse where this stock is held. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N WarehouseProductStock)

productId.description: Reference to the Product whose stock is being tracked. (Relationship: Product 1:N WarehouseProductStock)

quantity.description: The current stock quantity of the product in this warehouse.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the stock quantity for this product in this warehouse was last updated.

StockMovement.title: StockMovement
StockMovement.description: Records every individual change in product stock levels, providing a comprehensive audit trail for inventory adjustments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StockMovement entity.

movementDate.description: The date and time when the stock movement occurred.

productId.description: Reference to the Product affected by this movement. (Relationship: Product 1:N StockMovement)

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse where the stock movement took place. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N StockMovement)

quantityChange.description: The change in quantity for the product (positive for stock-in, negative for stock-out).

newStockLevel.description: The stock level of the product in the warehouse immediately after this movement occurred.

referenceId.description: Reference to the specific transaction (e.g., GoodsReceipt, GoodsIssue, Transfer) that caused this movement.

referenceType.description: The type of the entity referenced by 'referenceId' (e.g., 'GoodsReceipt', 'GoodsIssue', 'Transfer').

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User who initiated or was responsible for this stock movement. (Relationship: User 1:N StockMovement)

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records all significant user activities and system operations for auditing, security, and compliance purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entity.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the audit event occurred.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed the action. Can be null for system-generated actions. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

action.description: A description of the action performed (e.g., 'Warehouse Created', 'Product Updated', 'GoodsReceipt Processed').

entityType.description: The type of entity that was affected by the action (e.g., 'Warehouse', 'Product', 'GoodsReceipt').

entityId.description: The unique identifier of the entity that was affected by the action.

details.description: A stringified JSON object containing additional details about the change, such as old and new values.

ipAddress.description: The IP address from which the action originated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. The document ID (`{userId}`) matches Firebase Authentication's UID. While `roleId` is present for informational purposes, primary role-based access control is managed via separate role collections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Defines the system roles (e.g., Admin, Warehouse Manager). This collection holds role metadata and is not used for direct authorization checks in security rules.

params.name: roleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the role.

definition.description: A collection used for Role-Based Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document here with the user's UID grants 'Admin' privileges. This enables QAP-friendly `exists()` checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user, signifying their 'Admin' role.

definition.description: A collection used for Role-Based Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document here with the user's UID grants 'Warehouse Manager' privileges, allowing management of assigned warehouses. This enables QAP-friendly `exists()` checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user, signifying their 'Warehouse Manager' role.

definition.description: A collection used for Role-Based Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document here with the user's UID grants 'Operator' privileges, allowing stock in/out operations for assigned warehouses. This enables QAP-friendly `exists()` checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user, signifying their 'Operator' role.

definition.description: Stores details for each warehouse. The `responsibleUserId` field is used to grant specific management rights to a user for this warehouse.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the warehouse.

definition.description: Manages product classification categories. Accessible globally for read, with restricted write access for Admins/Warehouse Managers.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Contains details for all products. Global scope, read-only for most users, write access for Admins/Warehouse Managers.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Lists all product suppliers. Global scope, read-only for most users, write access for Admins/Warehouse Managers.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier for the supplier.

definition.description: Records goods received into a specific warehouse. The `warehouseId` is in the path. The `responsibleUserId` field is present for authorization and audit, allowing direct checks in rules for transaction ownership.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the warehouse where goods were received.

params.name: goodsReceiptId
params.description: The unique identifier for this goods receipt transaction.

definition.description: Details individual products received in a goods receipt. Includes denormalized 'warehouseId' (from parent path) and 'goodsReceiptResponsibleUserId' (copied from parent GoodsReceipt) for authorization independence.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the warehouse (denormalized from parent path).

params.name: goodsReceiptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent goods receipt.

params.name: goodsReceiptItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific item in the goods receipt.

definition.description: Records goods issued from a specific warehouse. The `warehouseId` is in the path. The `responsibleUserId` field is present for authorization and audit, allowing direct checks in rules for transaction ownership.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the warehouse from which goods were issued.

params.name: goodsIssueId
params.description: The unique identifier for this goods issue transaction.

definition.description: Details individual products issued in a goods issue. Includes denormalized 'warehouseId' (from parent path) and 'goodsIssueResponsibleUserId' (copied from parent GoodsIssue) for authorization independence.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the warehouse (denormalized from parent path).

params.name: goodsIssueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent goods issue.

params.name: goodsIssueItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific item in the goods issue.

definition.description: Records the movement of goods between two warehouses. For authorization independence, it includes denormalized 'sourceWarehouseResponsibleUserId' and 'destinationWarehouseResponsibleUserId' from the respective Warehouse documents, along with the 'responsibleUserId' of the transfer initiator.

params.name: transferId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transfer transaction.

definition.description: Details individual products involved in a transfer. Includes denormalized 'sourceWarehouseId', 'destinationWarehouseId', 'transferResponsibleUserId', 'sourceWarehouseResponsibleUserId', and 'destinationWarehouseResponsibleUserId' (all copied from parent Transfer) for authorization independence.

params.name: transferId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent transfer.

params.name: transferItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific item in the transfer.

definition.description: Stores the current stock quantity for a specific product within a specific warehouse. The `warehouseId` and `productId` are part of the path, ensuring efficient lookup and warehouse-scoped security checks.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the warehouse.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Logs every change in stock level for audit purposes. `warehouseId` is in the path, and `responsibleUserId` is in the document for direct authorization checks and historical tracking.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the warehouse where the movement occurred.

params.name: stockMovementId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific stock movement record.

definition.description: Records all significant user activities and system operations for auditing. This is a global collection, with read access typically restricted to Admin roles.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific audit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for OmniStock is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, debuggability, and compliance with QAPs (Queries Are Not Permissions). This is achieved through a multi-pronged approach involving structural segregation, explicit path-based ownership, and crucial denormalization of authorization context.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Denormalization for Warehouse-Scoped Operations**: All operational documents pertaining to a specific warehouse (e.g., `GoodsReceipts`, `GoodsIssues`, `StockMovements`) are organized as subcollections under `/warehouses/{warehouseId}`. This places the `warehouseId` directly in the document path, making it immediately available for security rule evaluation. For the corresponding item subcollections (e.g., `GoodsReceiptItems`, `GoodsIssueItems`), critical authorization data from their parent documents (such as the `warehouseId` and the `responsibleUserId` of the specific transaction) are **denormalized** into each item document. This eliminates the need for expensive and non-atomic `get()` calls in security rules to ascertain parent document permissions, ensuring atomic authorization checks and simplifying rule logic.
2.  **Denormalization for Cross-Warehouse Transfers**: Transfers are uniquely challenging as they involve two warehouses. To maintain authorization independence, the `Transfer` document at the root level `/transfers/{transferId}` explicitly **denormalizes** the `responsibleUserId` from both the `sourceWarehouse` and `destinationWarehouse` as `sourceWarehouseResponsibleUserId` and `destinationWarehouseResponsibleUserId`. This allows security rules to verify permissions for both ends of the transfer without requiring separate `get()` operations. Similarly, `TransferItem` documents further denormalize all relevant `warehouseId`s and `responsibleUserId`s from their parent `Transfer` document, ensuring complete authorization context is present locally.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **DBAC for Global Roles**: User roles are implemented using dedicated top-level collections (e.g., `/rolesAdmin/{userId}`, `/rolesWarehouseManager/{userId}`, `/rolesOperator/{userId}`). The mere existence of a document in one of these collections grants the associated role. Security rules can then use `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/rolesAdmin/$(request.auth.uid))` for direct, QAP-compliant role checks, avoiding client-side filtering or complex `get()` queries on user profiles.
2.  **Structural Segregation and Path-Based Ownership**: By nesting related data under clear, descriptive paths (e.g., `/users/{userId}/...` for private user data, or `/warehouses/{warehouseId}/...` for warehouse-specific data), the structure inherently enforces a security posture. This allows rules to leverage path wildcards (`{userId}`, `{warehouseId}`) and document fields (`resource.data.responsibleUserId`) to authorize `list` operations directly and efficiently. For example, a user can `list` `goodsReceipts` only within warehouses they are authorized for, as checked directly by the `warehouseId` in the path and the user's role/responsibility.

**Key Structural Choices:**
*   **Roles and Users**: `users/{userId}` stores user profiles, while global role collections like `rolesAdmin/{userId}` serve as the definitive source for role-based access control in security rules. `roles/{roleId}` provides metadata about roles.
*   **Warehouse-Centric Operations**: All operational data related to specific warehouse activities (`GoodsReceipts`, `GoodsIssues`, `StockMovements`, `WarehouseProductStocks`) are nested directly under `/warehouses/{warehouseId}/...`. This localizes data access and authorization.
*   **Global Entities**: `categories`, `products`, `suppliers`, and `auditLogs` are global collections, typically managed by specific roles (e.g., Admin) with broad read access for others.
*   **Explicit State Modeling**: The `Transfer` entity uses a clear `status` field for predictable state transitions.
*   **Predictable Schema & Consistency**: Strict naming conventions are applied (e.g., plural collection names, descriptive wildcards like `{warehouseId}`), ensuring clarity and maintainability. Authorization fields are standardized (e.g., `responsibleUserId`).
*   **Invariants**: `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `isDeleted` are consistently used for auditing and soft-deletion. Denormalized fields are explicitly managed to preserve data integrity across transactions for authorization purposes.

This robust design ensures that OmniStock's Firestore security rules are easy to write, reason about, and debug, while supporting high-performance queries and transactional integrity.
